headline: A Look At Estée Lauder (EL) Valuation As Tariff And Travel Retail Risks Cloud Fiscal 2026 Outlook
summary: Estée Lauder Companies (EL) is back in focus after management highlighted tariff and travel retail risks in its fiscal 2026 second half outlook, just as rising oil prices and geopolitical tensions unsettled broader markets. See our latest analysis for Estée Lauder Companies. The recent tariff and travel retail warnings came on top of a sharp de-rating in the last few sessions, with the share price falling 7.92% in a day and 21.05% year to date. This comes even though the 1 year total...
